mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation through a lower discount rate. When policy is actively easing, the S&P 500 should post positive 20-day forward returns because present values of expected cash flows rise faster than earnings expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are not positive in a majority of months during FEDFUNDS downtrends, or if the sign of the effect is negative in any 3-year rolling window with at least 30 triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent path signals weakening household income and deteriorating risk appetite, which should depress equity prices over the next month. The mechanism is falsified if unemployment increases without subsequent negative SPX returns.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment jumps are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average 20-day return after triggers is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized upswing in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad domestic demand strength in consumer spending and housing activity. This combination should support cyclical earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-growth state of RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day returns, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 55% over any 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating INDPRO signals rising capacity utilization and stronger industrial earnings momentum, which should lift the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when industrial output is rising from an already positive base.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth turns positive after at least 2 months of weakness
failure_condition: Reject if post-bottom INDPRO upturns fail to produce above-baseline positive 20-day returns, or if the conditional average return is not significantly greater than zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPIAUCSL above recent inflation momentum is negative for equities because it raises real discount rates and increases the odds of tighter policy. The effect should be visible within one month and vanish when inflation remains stable.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds prior 6-month average by 0.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation acceleration episodes are not followed by lower-than-normal 20-day returns, or if the effect disappears after conditioning on FEDFUNDS while CPIAUCSL still triggers.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent PAYEMS growth reflects continuing payroll expansion and stronger aggregate income, which should support equity demand and earnings revisions over a longer horizon. When employment growth is sustained, the S&P 500 should be higher 60 trading days later.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month payroll expansion episodes fail to produce positive 60-day returns more often than not, or if the median 60-day return after triggers is not above the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above a restrictive threshold and CPIAUCSL is still accelerating, the market should price a continued policy squeeze and lower equity multiples. This interaction should predict negative returns over the next month, not either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint restrictive-rate and rising-inflation state does not produce negative 20-day returns, or if the interaction term is not stronger than each standalone proxy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST combined with weak RSAFS identifies demand shock conditions that usually precede equity drawdowns through falling sales expectations and housing-led sentiment deterioration. The signal is expected to work over a quarter rather than just a month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average and RSAFS growth turns negative
failure_condition: Reject if joint housing-sales weakness does not predict negative 60-day returns, or if the conditional hit rate is below 40% over any 5-year rolling sample.
